structured_payload_candidate.title: Room Temperature Control Method Restriction
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The room temperature control method should only be used in non -occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).

evidence_quote: The room temperature control method should only be used in non -occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).
evidence_text: The room temperature control method should only be used in non -occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Maintenance Interval Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm when fan run hours exceed 3,000. Reset the interval counter when the alarm is acknowledged.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for mo re than 3,000 hours:  Level 4.  Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for mo re than 3,000 hours:  Level 4.  Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
evidence_text: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for mo re than 3,000 hours:  Level 4.  Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
judge_reason: Grounded fault diagnostic rule from Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Status Mismatch Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Alarm if fan status differs from command for 15 seconds. Commanded on/status off is Level 2; commanded off/status off is Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: fan command != fan status for 15 seconds
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2 b. Commanded off, status off:  Level 4

evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2 b. Commanded off, status off:  Level 4
evidence_text: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2 b. Commanded off, status off:  Level 4
judge_reason: Grounded fault diagnostic rule from Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Exhaust Fan Start/Stop - Room Temperature Cycling
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint and stop when zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: exhaust fan runs; OFF when zone temperature < active cooling setpoint - 1°C (2°F) for 2 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.

evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.
evidence_text: Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Exhaust Fan Start/Stop - Zone Group Occupancy
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: any associated system supply fan proven on AND any associated Zone Group in Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group  is in the Occupied Mode.

evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group  is in the Occupied Mode.
evidence_text: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group  is in the Occupied Mode.
